skip_reason: No audience-cluster surfaces on this homepage. Five baton sections route to visual-cta, category-navigation, product-media, pricing, trust-credibility, post-purchase, content-seo — none to audience.
notes: Page is a confirmed US domestic market homepage (hreflang: [], USD pricing, 'Contiguous US only' free shipping banner, English-only content). Cross-cultural reference file contributes nothing. Single-market domestic edge case per audience cluster guidance — absent localization signals are not a defect.

Capture state is anonymous / logged-out (no auth_state in baton capture_state). The absence of personalization on a logged-out homepage is a deliberate stance, not a defect, per audience cluster guidance: 90-98% of e-commerce traffic is anonymous (personalization-psychology.md Finding 9). Only the vehicle selector ('SELECT YOUR VEHICLE') offers a first-party personalization entry point — a positive signal that the lead has appropriately routed elsewhere (category-navigation / performance-ux).

Social commerce surface is absent: no TikTok Shop, Instagram Shopping, UGC gallery, or creator embeds. The product category (automotive performance and styling mods, $20-$1,800+ AOV) does not call for social-native in-app checkout; absent social commerce integration is not a defect for this product/demographic fit. Social presence exists (Facebook, Instagram, YouTube links in footer schema and DOM), but no shoppable or community-proof embeds appear on the homepage.

Newsletter section (e35) uses vehicle-community-segmented copy ('Focus RS, Focus ST, WRX, or STI') — a light audience-recognition signal that acknowledges specific community membership. This is a positive audience alignment touch; not a finding, but worth noting for synthesizer context.
No personalized pricing, algorithmic transparency labels, or surveillance-adjacent signals detected. Page is clean on personalization-psychology.md risk factors.
